title: Sequences, Recurrences, and Discrete Change · Evidence Workshop
summary: Forecast savings, inventory, and staged population processes. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a discrete recurrence forecast while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent explicit and recursive sequences and state valid index domains.
Model constant differences and constant ratios with arithmetic and geometric sequences.
Calculate finite sums and explain their accumulated quantity and units.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Produce a checked model result
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 21 units, changes by −8 units, then by +6 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect long-run behavior and sensitivity of discrete models.
